docs.title: COMPASS Documentation
docs.text: COMPASS (Crew Operations Management & Personnel Assignment Support System) is the official platform for STARFLEET International chapter management in Region 3 and beyond.

docs.title: Where do you want to start?
docs.text:
• CO Quick Start — New to COMPASS? Get your ship set up.
  • Roster Guide — Add crew, manage transfers, update ranks.
  • Promotions Guide — Submit promotions and track requirements.
  • Events Guide — Log meetings and track attendance.

docs.title: What is COMPASS?
docs.text:
COMPASS replaces the legacy Access database with a modern, multi-ship platform giving COs real-time visibility into crew activity and promotion progress. It manages crew rosters, promotions, events, awards, academy records, and announcements.

COMPASS is a supplemental platform — it works alongside the SFI Members Portal (db.sfi.org), not as a replacement.

docs.title: Need Help?
docs.text: Check the relevant guide's Common Issues section, or contact your Regional Coordinator.

docs.title: Commanding Officer Guide
docs.text: This guide covers everything a CO needs to run their ship effectively in COMPASS.

docs.title: In This Guide
docs.text: Section What You'll Learn Getting Started First login, ship setup, adding your first crew members Managing Your Roster Adding, editing, transferring, and retiring crew Promotions Submitting promotions, requirements, the approval workflow Events Creating events, recording attendance, activity reporting Awards & Nominations Nominating crew, award categories, permanent points

docs.title: Your Role in COMPASS
docs.text:
As CO, you are the primary administrator for your ship's data. Your XO shares most permissions and can act on your behalf.

Keep It Current

COMPASS is most useful when data is entered promptly. A roster updated weekly takes about 5 minutes. A roster updated quarterly takes hours to reconcile.

docs.title: Awards & Nominations
docs.text: COMPASS manages the full awards workflow from CO nominations through regional approval and permanent point awards.

docs.title: Nominating a Crew Member
docs.text:
Go to Awards → Submit Nomination. You'll need the member, award, citation, and date of action.

Write a Good Citation

Be specific. "For organizing three consecutive monthly meetings during the CO's LOA, maintaining 90% crew attendance" is far stronger than "for outstanding service."

docs.title: Award Categories
docs.text:
Category Examples Service Awards Length of service, years of membership Achievement Awards Specific accomplishments, milestones Command Awards CO/XO excellence, ship performance Recruitment Awards Bringing in new members Academic Awards Academy course completions

Each award's point value is applied automatically to the member's permanent points on approval.

docs.title: Approval Workflow
docs.text:
CO submits → Regional review → Approved → Points awarded

Track pending nominations under Awards → Pending.

docs.title: Annual Awards
docs.text: COMPASS manages the SFI annual award cycle separately. Nominations are submitted during the cycle window, reviewed regionally, and EC-level awards are voted on by the Executive Committee. Your RC will announce when the window opens.

docs.title: Common Issues
docs.text:
Award not in the catalog. Contact your RC — the catalog is managed at the regional/fleet level.

Points didn't update after approval. Refresh the member's profile. If still wrong, contact the platform admin.

docs.title: Events
docs.text: Event tracking builds the activity record that drives promotion eligibility, award nominations, and monthly reports. Every meeting or activity should be logged.

docs.title: Creating an Event
docs.text:
Go to Events → Create Event.

Required: event name, event type, date, attending crew.

docs.title: Event Types
docs.text:
Type Examples Monthly Meeting Regular ship meetings Ship Activity Social events, watch parties, game nights Convention Regional Summit, local cons Community Service Charity work, outreach Training/Academy Study groups, test prep

Point values per type are configured at the regional level.

docs.title: Recording Attendance
docs.text:
Check each member who attended when creating the event. You can edit attendance afterward via Events → [Event Name] → Edit Attendance.

Tip

Enter attendance the same day. Remembering who attended two weeks later is much harder.

docs.title: Common Issues
docs.text:
Forgot to log an event from last month. Enter it with the correct past date — no problem.

A member's attendance isn't showing. Open the event and verify they're checked in the attendance list.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This guide walks you through setting up your ship in COMPASS from scratch. The entire process takes about five minutes and is largely automatic — COMPASS pulls your ship data and crew roster directly from the SFI Members Portal.

docs.title: Before You Begin
docs.text:
You'll need:

  • Your db.sfi.org username and password (your SFI database credentials — not a COMPASS password)
  • To be listed as CO or XO of your chapter in db.sfi.org — the setup will fail if you're not

These are your SFI credentials, not a COMPASS account

COMPASS uses your db.sfi.org credentials once to pull your chapter info and roster. They are never stored. If you don't have db.sfi.org credentials, log in at db.sfi.org first to set them up.

docs.title: Step 1 — Enter Your SFI Credentials
docs.text:
Go to compass.sfi.org/ship-setup.

You'll see the Add Your Ship to COMPASS screen asking for your db.sfi.org username and password.

Enter your credentials and click Connect to SFI. COMPASS will authenticate against the SFI database and fetch your chapter information and roster. This takes a few seconds — a loading spinner will appear while it works.

Only CO or XO can run setup

COMPASS verifies your role against db.sfi.org. If you're not listed as CO or XO, you'll see an error: "Only the Commanding Officer or Executive Officer can set up a ship in COMPASS." Have your CO or XO run the setup instead.

Common errors at this step:

  • "Unable to authenticate" — double-check your db.sfi.org username and password. Log into db.sfi.org directly first to confirm they work.
  • "Unable to retrieve chapter information" — your account authenticated but has no CO/XO role on file. Contact your RC.

docs.title: Step 2 — Select Your Chapter (if prompted)
docs.text:
If your SFI account is associated with more than one chapter, COMPASS will show you a chapter selection screen. Pick the chapter you're setting up and continue.

If you only have one chapter, this step is skipped automatically.

docs.title: Step 3 — Review Your Ship Information
docs.text:
COMPASS will display a Review & Confirm page showing everything it pulled from db.sfi.org:

Ship Information

  • Ship name and registry
  • Ship class
  • Region
  • Chapter type
  • Commissioned date and charter city

Command Staff

  • CO name and SCC — with a "You" badge next to your name
  • XO name and SCC

Crew Roster Preview

A table showing every member COMPASS found in your db.sfi.org roster, with their position, rank, name, and SCC. This is a preview — nothing has been saved yet.

Review this carefully. If the ship name, registry, or command staff looks wrong, click ← Start Over and contact your RC — the data comes directly from db.sfi.org, so any errors need to be corrected there first.

Ship already exists in COMPASS

If your ship has already been set up by someone else, you'll see an orange warning on this page and the Create Ship button won't appear. Contact your RC to get access to your ship's existing record.

docs.title: Step 4 — Create Ship & Import Crew
docs.text:
If everything looks correct, click Create Ship & Import Crew.

COMPASS will:

  1. Create your ship record — name, registry, class, region, and all chapter details
  2. Import your entire crew roster — all members from db.sfi.org are added to COMPASS with their ranks, positions, and SCC numbers
  3. Create your COMPASS account — you're automatically logged in after setup
  4. Sync your eligibility data — your OTS/OCC completions and academy records are pulled from db.sfi.org

Don't close the page

A loading spinner appears while provisioning runs. This can take 10–15 seconds depending on roster size. Don't close or refresh the page while it's working.

docs.title: Step 5 — You're In
docs.text:
On success, COMPASS redirects you to the Ship Dashboard with a confirmation message:

"Welcome to COMPASS! USS [Your Ship] (NCC-XXXXX) has been successfully set up. Imported X new crew and updated Y existing."

Your ship is now live in COMPASS with your full roster already imported.

docs.title: After Setup — What to Do Next
docs.text: Your ship is operational, but there are a few things worth doing immediately:

docs.title: Set Your Password
docs.text: Your COMPASS account was created automatically with a temporary password. Go to Profile → Change Password and set a real one before logging out.

docs.title: Verify Your Roster
docs.text:
Go to Crew and scan through your imported roster. Check that:

  • All active members are present
  • Ranks look correct
  • Anyone who has left SFI or transferred is marked accordingly (see Managing Your Roster)

The import pulls directly from db.sfi.org, so if someone is missing it means they weren't on your chapter's roster there.

docs.title: Invite Your XO
docs.text: Your XO was imported as a crew member but may not have a COMPASS account yet. Direct them to compass.sfi.org to register — they'll need to create an account with the same SCC on file. Once their account is active, their CO/XO permissions will apply automatically.

docs.title: Log Your First Event
docs.text: Go to Events → Create Event and enter your most recent ship meeting. See the Events Guide.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Setup failed partway through. The setup runs as a single transaction — if anything fails, nothing is saved. You can safely run it again from the start. Check the error message for specifics and contact your RC if it persists.

My roster shows 0 members. Your chapter may not have a roster on file in db.sfi.org, or there may be a formatting issue with the data. Your ship record will still be created — you can add crew manually afterward through Crew → Add Member.

I already have a COMPASS account but I'm running setup. That's fine — COMPASS will detect your existing account (matched by SCC) and link it to the new ship rather than creating a duplicate.

My ship was previously in COMPASS but was withdrawn. COMPASS handles this automatically. If your ship's registry matches a withdrawn ship in the system, setup will reactivate it rather than create a duplicate. Your historical data (awards, promotion history, events) is fully preserved. Crew members won't be auto-restored — they'll need to rejoin through the normal join request process.

docs.title: Promotions
docs.text: COMPASS manages the full promotion workflow from eligibility checking through SFI database rank update.

docs.title: How Promotions Work
docs.text: CO submits → Regional review → RC approval → SFI DB rank update

docs.title: Checking Promotion Eligibility
docs.text: Go to Crew → [Member Name] → Promotions Tab to see current rank, time-in-grade, permanent points, and outstanding requirements. COMPASS calculates eligibility automatically.

docs.title: Submitting a Promotion
docs.text:
Go to Promotions → Submit New Promotion. You'll need the member, proposed rank, a justification narrative, and effective date.

Deferred Promotions

You can set a future effective date — COMPASS holds it and applies automatically. Useful for promotions announced at events.

docs.title: Approval Workflow
docs.text:
Stage Who Acts Submitted CO Regional Review RC or RCOS Approved RC SFI DB Update Submitted to db.sfi.org

Track pending promotions under Promotions → Pending.

docs.title: Flag Officer Promotions (O-7+)
docs.text:
• O-7 / O-8 — Require Chief of Starfleet (CS) direct approval
  • O-9+ — Require Executive Committee vote and Promotions Director review

COMPASS generates a nomination PDF for these. Your RC will guide you through the process.

docs.title: Common Issues
docs.text:
Member shows ineligible but should qualify. Check that all event attendance and awards are fully entered — missing data is the most common cause.

Need to retract a submission. Contact your RC once a promotion has left ship-level review.

docs.title: Managing Your Roster
docs.text: Your crew roster is the foundation of everything in COMPASS.

docs.title: Viewing Your Roster
docs.text: Go to Crew to see your full ship roster with name, rank, SCC, status, permanent points, and last activity date.

docs.title: Member Statuses
docs.text: Status Meaning Active Full member, counts toward all reports Reserve Limited participation, still on roster LOA Leave of Absence — temporarily inactive Inactive No current participation Transferred Moved to another ship Retired Left SFI or the chapter

docs.title: Adding a Crew Member
docs.text:
Go to Crew → Add Member. Required: SCC, full name, rank, status. Recommended: email address, join date.

Duplicate SCCs

COMPASS won't allow two members with the same SCC. If you get a duplicate error, check with your RC.

docs.title: Editing a Crew Member
docs.text: Click any member's name to open their profile, then click Edit to make changes.

docs.title: Updating Ranks
docs.text:
Note

SFI database rank always takes priority. If there's a discrepancy, db.sfi.org is the source of truth.

docs.title: Transferring a Member
docs.text:
1. Open their profile
  2. Go to Actions → Transfer Member
  3. Select the destination ship and confirm

Transferred members show as pending on the receiving ship until the CO confirms.

docs.title: Roster Best Practices
docs.text:
• Audit monthly before submitting reports
  • Enter new members promptly — don't wait until report time
  • Use LOA for members who intend to return, not Inactive
  • Don't delete members — change their status instead

docs.title: Common Issues
docs.text:
I added a member but they can't log in. They need to register at compass.sfi.org separately.

A member appears on two ships. Contact your RC to resolve.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
CO — Commanding Officer. The ship's captain and primary COMPASS administrator.

COMPASS — Crew Operations Management & Personnel Assignment Support System.

db.sfi.org — The SFI Members Portal. COMPASS supplements but does not replace it.

EC — Executive Committee. SFI's governing body at the fleet level.

LOA — Leave of Absence. A temporary inactive status for members who intend to return.

OCC — Officer Candidate Course. An academy track for officer advancement.

OTS — Officer Training School. Entry-level officer training tracked in permanent points.

Permanent Points — Cumulative point total driving promotion eligibility. Earned through events, awards, academy completions, and leadership positions.

RC — Regional Coordinator. The regional administrator responsible for a group of ships.

RCOS — Regional Chief of Staff. Assists the RC in regional operations.

Region 3 — The SFI region including USS Veritas, USS Comanche, USS Corsair, USS Zavala, USS Joan of Arc, and USS Battle Born.

SCC — Star Cluster Certification (member number). Unique identifier for every SFI member.

SFI — STARFLEET International. The fan organization COMPASS serves.

SFMC — Star Fleet Marine Corps. SFI's marine auxiliary, managed through a dedicated COMPASS module.

XO — Executive Officer. The ship's first officer; shares most CO permissions in COMPASS.

docs.title: Rank Chart
docs.text: Code Rank Abbreviation O-1 Ensign ENS O-2 Lieutenant Junior Grade LTJG O-3 Lieutenant LT O-4 Lieutenant Commander LCDR O-5 Commander CDR O-6 Captain CAPT O-7 Rear Admiral RADM O-8 Vice Admiral VADM O-9 Admiral ADM O-10 Fleet Admiral FADM

docs.title: Promotion Notes
docs.text:
• O-1 through O-6 — Standard ship/regional workflow
  • O-7 / O-8 — Require Chief of Starfleet (CS) direct approval
  • O-9+ — Require Executive Committee vote and Promotions Director review

Note

For the authoritative rank structure, refer to the SFI Member Handbook.
